message:
feat(pt-core): Implement deep_scan with full /proc introspection

Implement comprehensive deep_scan module for detailed process analysis
on Linux via /proc filesystem:

DeepScanRecord structure containing:
- Core identity: pid, ppid, uid, user, pgid, sid, start_id
- Process info: comm, cmd, state, exe_path
- Timing: start_time_unix, elapsed
- Resource usage: cpu_percent, rss_bytes, vsz_bytes
- I/O stats: IoStats (rchar, wchar, read_bytes, write_bytes, etc.)
- Scheduler stats: SchedStats (cpu_time_ns, wait_time_ns, timeslices)
- Memory stats: MemStats (size, resident, shared, text pages)
- File descriptors: Vec<FdInfo> with socket detection
- Cgroup info: Vec<CgroupInfo> for container detection
- Wait channel: wchan for blocking analysis
- Container detection: is_containerized heuristic

DeepScanOptions:
- pids: Target specific processes or scan all
- skip_inaccessible: Skip processes with permission issues
- include_environ: Include environment variables

DeepScanResult with metadata:
- Process count, duration, warnings
- Platform and boot_id for correlation

Also fixes proc_parsers.rs sched parsing to handle lines without
colons gracefully (skip headers instead of early return).

Exports DeepScanMetadata and DeepScanResult from collect module.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
